title: Iran Data Portal (Syracuse) — Mean age at first marriage by sex, registered marriages/divorces, registered births, and settled/unsettled (nomadic) population by province, from SCI census/vital-registration series
source_org: Iran Data Portal, Syracuse University (Persian source: Statistical Centre of Iran, national population & housing censuses and civil registration)

files.name: 10.-mean-age-at-first-marriage-by-sex-1345-1385-census-1966-2006.xlsx
files.notes: Mean age at first marriage by sex, national/urban/rural, 6 census years 1345-1385 SH (1966, 1976, 1986, 1991, 1996, 2006) -- male age falls 25.0->23.6 then rises to ~25.6 by 1996 (need full read for 2006 row); female age rises steadily 18.4 (1966) -> 22.4 (1996), the classic economic-demographic marker of rising female education/labor participation

files.name: 12-settled-and-unsettled-population-and-households-of-ostans-1385-2006-census.xlsx
files.notes: 2006 census: population/households broken into settled-urban / settled-rural / UNSETTLED (Iran census terminology for nomadic/pastoral population) by all 31 provinces (ostans) plus national total. National total unsettled: 21,321 households / 104,717 persons -- much lower than the ~1.3M 'nomad' figure from the dedicated 1987/2008 Nomad Censuses (see SOURCES.md notes), because the general population census's 'unsettled' category only catches nomads actually enumerated while migrating/outside settled villages at census time, undercounting true pastoral-nomad population which SCI's separate specialized Ashayer (Nomad) Census counts more completely -- both figures are recorded, with this caveat, since they answer different questions (census-day unsettled headcount vs. total nomadic-lifestyle population)

files.name: 22-registered-births-by-sex-1370-1385-1991-2006.xlsx
files.notes: Registered births by sex, annual, 1991-2006, national/urban/rural

files.name: 23-registered-marriages-and-divorces-1370-1385-1991-2006.xlsx
files.notes: Registered marriages and divorces, annual, 1991-2006, national/urban/rural -- divorce rate is itself a useful economic-demographic indicator (correlates with female economic independence)

frequency: census-year (marriage age, settlement) / annual (births, marriages, divorces)
units: years (mean age at marriage); persons/households (population tables); counts (births/marriages/divorces)
license_terms: Iran Data Portal / SCI — academic redistribution, no restrictive license stated
notes: Directly answers project brief bucket 3 (age at first marriage trends, a well-documented economic-demographic indicator) with a genuine 1966-2006 multi-decade series, and partially answers bucket 2 (nomadic population) via the settled/unsettled 2006 provincial breakdown, cross-referenced against the dedicated 1987/2008 Nomad Census figures found separately this round (see SOURCES.md Round 38) which are the better nomad-specific series -- this table adds province-level granularity the dedicated censuses' summary reporting doesn't provide in the sources found.
